Step 	3: 	The 	Big Problem 	(Part 1)

Jon: 	Okay, welcome to the tutorial presentation step 3 of 5 in the 5 step
VSL process, and this is the big problem.
Now, let’s review the 5 steps in order, as we always do.
We’ve got the snap suggestion, step one, should have been through
with that. Step 2, the vital connection, should be through with that.
We’re at the big problem. The bigger solution comes next, and the
grand offer.
Those are the 5 steps of creating a VSL. So, step 3 is the big problem.
This is drawing your prospect’s attention to the source of their problem
and that source is greater than their pain. What do I mean by that?
I’m going to make it very clear.
This all takes place within the first few slides of the big problem, and
this is centered around the concept of creating a conspiracy. And
some service-oriented products believe that they don’t have a
conspiracy. We have yet to see anyone that doesn’t have a
conspiracy associated with their product or service.
A conspiracy is a bit of fun, by the way. We’re not trying to literally
create a conspiracy that’s some sort of a global phenomenon that’s
going to go viral or anything like that, we’re just going to make an
enemy bigger than the problem itself, because that’s going to make
your reader go into a different frame of mind. It’s going to remove guilt,
it’s going to remove that concept of. “This is my fault, I’ve done all this
wrong, I’m stupid, I don’t get it,” and those kind of things.
We’re going to use the conspiracy theory model to take that away.
And what that does, is open them up to actually take action. Because
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 9 --

2
once you’ve empowered someone to say, “Hey, I’m not as slow as I
thought I was. I think I can see the big picture here, I see the problem,
it’s not really with me, there’s other stuff that’s contributing to this.”
So, they feel more empowered to what? To take action, to make a
decision. And one of the things you want them to take action on, is
clicking your buy button. This is why create a conspiracy is a big deal.
Let’s go over the conspiracy cycle, how you create a conspiracy. And
this, again, is the foundation of the big problem.
Your prospect does not just have a problem. You want to frame this in
a way where your prospect understands that he or she is involved in a
conspiracy. It’s something much bigger than their problem. And a
conspiracy, again, is the simple source, it’s the source of your
prospect’s withouts.
Now, what do I mean by that? Refer back to assignment in part 3 of
the snap suggestion.
You have an assignment in part 3, in the snap suggestion, and it’s
creating withouts. All you do is take your benefits, and you where to
create 2 to 3 withouts. So if I say, “You get X benefit without all the
pain, all the suffering, and all the torture.” Let’s say that was my 3
withouts. Well, those withouts are the source, they’re what you’re
going to use to create the lies and the conspiracy that you’re about to
share with your viewer.
This is very, very important. You’ve already done the assignment, so
good for you. Pull it up. You’re going to need to, because you will.
And this is where you’re going to get all the material. You don’t have
to create anything now, because you’ve already done it.
Now, this is a calling that’s larger than the prospect’s problem; it’s what
I call cause over effect. Rather than cause and effect, this is cause
over effect. The cause of the problem is much more important and
much greater than the effect of the problem on the user. This creates
a stronger desire, psychologically, to take action.
Now, here are the steps that we’re going to follow to create your
conspiracy cycle.
Step one, the big lie. Step 2, it’s not your fault. Step 3, blame this.
Step 4, the big truth. And step 5, open the bigger solution loop.
We’re going to go through each of these steps in detail.
Step one, the big lie. Here’s part one of this: do you need to discover
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 9 --

3
your lie? What is your lie? Well we’re going to show you where your
lie is. Like I said, it’s already found within your withouts.
You’re going to transition into that lie, you’re going to describe the lie,
you’re going to label it as false.
And by the way, these parts may sound like, “Oh, my gosh, this is
really complicated.” Each of these is very simple. They’re very short.
We just wanted to break it and chunk it down for you.
So, you’re going to label it as false, you’re going to tell them what the
results are or have been when they believed this lie, and then you’re
going to transition out of it. You’re going to tell them, “This is not what I
want for you.”
Let’s dig into part one, discovering your lies. You’re going to begin by
going to your assignment for the snap suggestion part 3. I want you to
reference that. In this assignment, you listed benefits, with 2 to 3
withouts.
So, this is something positive that’s associated to your product, without
2 to 3 negative things. Give you some examples here. These withouts
are actually the lies, by the way, that your prospect may believe.
And listen, this is going to be very important for you to understand.
Your prospect may believe that they actually need to do these lies in
order to see results, but they don’t. They’re lies. They’re
misinformation. This is what I want you to really come away with from
this video tutorial, is that the lies and the withouts are the
misinformation. And let me give you an example right here, before we
move on.
For example, if one of my benefits of my product was that you can
make money online, let’s say that was the big driving benefit.
Now, that’s not a very good benefit. Obviously, we’ve gone over how
to word your benefits already. But let’s say my benefit was, I’m going
to make more money online, “You can make more money online,
without driving to work in traffic, without having money in the bank to
start with, and without,” whatever.
Let’s take the second without. Without having money in the bank to
start with. That is a lie that they may believe, people that are watching
your video may believe, “Yeah, but I have to have money to start with
to make money online.” So, you’re going to tell them why that’s a lie,
and then you’re going to build a conspiracy around that lie. This is
basically it.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 9 --

4
Let me give you a specific example. This is one of my lies, in my
specific product example with the Every Other Day Diet. “Without
starving yourself skinny.” So I am telling someone that they can lose
weight, without starving yourself skinny. They can eat their favorite
foods without starving themselves skinny.
So, here’s how I might word that on a slide. “Many people believe that
you have to starve yourself skinny in order to have the body your
desire.” It’s that simple. This is how I would word the lie. And then, I
would go on with the next steps in that. We’re going to do that in just a
moment. This is my first lie.
Now I will use it after part 2, the transition into the lie. So, I just wanted
to give you an example slide, right now. But you don’t start off with
this; you need to transition into the concept of lying first.
But I wanted to show you how I formed one, so that you’d have a
mental picture of what we’re doing here in the following steps.
So, I’ve discovered my lie. What is my lie?
Well, my lie is “Without starving.” That’s the lie. People believe that
they may be starving themselves, or they have to starve themselves to
be skinny. So, what’s your first lie? Well, it’s found within your without
exercises.
So, find the 2 to 3 withouts that your created, there’s your lies. That’s
how you discover your lie.
Now, part 2 is the transition into the lie. I’ve given you an example
slide here. Here’s how I would go into it. “The real problem is the fact
that you’ve been lied to for years, and these lies are keeping you from
the body you desire and you deserve.”
There’s a lot of wordsmithing going on here. “The real problem,” notice
that, that’s going to jump right out at you because I’ve chosen to
highlight that in red, “is the fact,” and this is a good wordsmithing tip, by
the way, don’t say, “Is that you’ve been lied to for years,” say “it’s the
fact,” or “the proven fact,” because it is. It’s a fact, so people are more
apt to believe that. “That you’ve been lied to for years. And these
lies,” there’s more than one.
Again, Again we’re going to create more than one lie, unless you don’t
have more than one lie, then it’s fine to use one. But most people
have 2 to 3 lies that they can share with their users. And they say,
“You may believe this, but it’s not true. And you may also believe this,
and that’s not true either.”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 9 --

5
You’re going to get to the truth in the bigger solution. “And look at
what these lies are doing, they’re keeping you from the body that you
desire and deserve.”
So, in other words, I didn’t say keeping you from losing weight, I said
keeping you from the body you desire and deserve. Desire and
deserve are what? Power words. Those are very enticing power
words.
So, just a little bit of wordsmithing on each slide. We’re going to go
into more specific examples later, but just wanted to give you one right
here. That’s the transition.
Now, I’m going to describe the lie. This is where you use your data
from part one. You already know what it is, so I’ve already given you
what my lie is, and this is where you put it. So I’ve got the transition,
and then I go right into it.
“One of these lies is that you have to starve yourself skinny. I bet
you’ve heard that, or maybe even believed that this at one point in your
life.”
Now, let’s look at what I’m doing here. I’m going to go into a little more
detail on this later, but I want you to look at the wordsmithing here.
“One of the lies,” I set that up because I know I’m going to have more
than one, “is that you have to starve yourself skinny.” You already
know what that lie is, because you’ve already determined it in the very
first part of this exercise. “I bet you’ve heard that, or maybe even
believed that.”
Remember our exercise on don’t assume? Do not assume things to
be factually true. You want to leave as much open-endedness as you
possibly can, until you get to the parts where you’re going to just tell
your user what to do, and that’s more in the grand offer.
So I say, “I bet you’ve heard that before, or maybe even believed this
at one point in your life.” So, I don’t exclude anyone in that statement.
If someone never heard that or if someone never believed that, than
they’re going, “Oh, well I didn’t, but he said maybe.” Your
subconscious is going to pick up that word “maybe” or “I bet.”
Then I anchor, what is this called? What is the bottom word of this
called? You should know, it’s called an anchor drop. Remember?
Anchor drop with the word “yet,” and the reason we do that is because
no one will bomb out on a slide. They will not quit watching your video
when you say the word “yet,” and you just leave someone on a
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 9 --

6
cliffhanger. So, I am forward-pacing them into the next slide.
So, some great wordsmithing techniques in there. But that is how I
describe the lie.
Now, you can describe the lie anyway you want. You can simply just
say the lie itself. If you wanted to, you could say, “A lot of people
believe that you have to starve yourself skinny,” and that’s all you have
to say.
But if you want to wordsmith it a little bit, I wanted to give you a little
extra.
Now again, notice the anchor drop on the word “yet.” And this creates
that forward momentum that you need to inspire continuation.
Video sales letters is all about continuation. It’s what makes them so
much more powerful than text-based sales pages, because you can
inspire continuation. You can get people to keep reading and keep
watching. You can’t do that very well on a text-based sales page.
Part 4 is simply to label it as false. You’re just going to negate it.
You’re just going to say, “That’s not true.” And this is as simple as this,
“You know, that’s not even true,” or, “That’s not even close to the
truth.” That’s just wordsmithing. If you don’t want to be a wordsmith,
you can say, “You know, that’s not true,” that’s it.
But I say, “That’s not even close to the truth.” And notice the words
that I’ve put in red. Yeah, remember in the 3X VSL toolbox, we cover
subliminal highlighting, and this is subliminal highlighting. You’ll see it
in a lot of slides that I gave you, and you’ll see it through the entire
template.
So I’ll give you hints on where to drop those subliminal highlighting.
Because, what are they reading here? The truth. I want them to start
embedding certain things into the viewers mind. The truth, buy, now,
act, those kind of things.
Part 5 is the results of your user having believed this lie. And this is
where you want to paint a vivid picture using the future-pacing
technique that we’ve already covered, and you’re going to create a
mini nightmare story for them, to describe the consequence of
believing this lie.
Here’s some example slides. I just put these in several slides within
the presentation, so I can walk you through this. “If you where one of
the millions who feel victim to this lie, then you must decide now, to
believe the truth instead, because,” and look what I’ve done here.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 9 --

7
I’m going to dissect each of these slides because I think it will really
help you. “If you are one of the millions,” what is that? That is an
inclusion statement. These are all in the 3X VSL toolbox. Inclusion
statements. You want to be part of a club. You want to draw users
into being part of a family, part of a community.
Well, “You’re one of the millions who fell victim to this lie,” that makes
them feel a lot less stupid, doesn’t it? A lot less ill-educated, a lot less
guilty. That’s what you want. This is rapport building and it’s very
important to do.
“If you’re one of the millions who feel victim to this lie, than you must
decide now.” Notice that I’ve used subliminal highlighting there.
“Decide now.” This is all gearing someone up for the buy, for the
grand offer, “to believe the truth instead, because,” anchor drop, “if you
do not,” and this is where I start painting the vivid picture.
Notice how everything is going to be in the present tense. “You will
continue down the road of the yoyo diet cycle. Your body’s
metabolism slows to a snail’s pace, halting all of your weight loss dead
in its tracks. And eventually, no matter how strong you are, you simply
give up on dieting, and you may even give up on ever achieving your
dream body.”
Okay, you see what I’ve done here? I’ve painted a very short
nightmare story of the results of believing that lie. If someone believes
they have to starve themselves skinny, then here’s what’s going to
happen. All this bad stuff’s going to happen, you’re going to shut down
your metabolism, you’re going to be a victim of yoyo dieting, and you
may just probably give up, no matter how strong you are.
And I want to really emphasis this point. “No matter how strong you
are.” Notice the word strong is highlighted. I am telling the user what
they are. I am telling the user what they are and what I want them to
do. Those are 2 criteria that I use for most of my subliminal
highlighting techniques.
They are strong and I want them to take action. Believe the truth,
strong, etc. You see where I’ve been highlighting these words? This
is the effect of believing the lie.
Part 6 is simply you saying, “I don’t want this for you. This is not what I
have in mind for you.” This is very important, because this deepens
your rapport even further. And all you need to use is a variation on the
phrase “this is not what I want for you.”
When someone tells that to you, let’s say a friend of yours says, “I
noticed that you’re going through a really bad phase with fill-in-the-
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 7 of 9 --

8
blank, and that’s not what I want for you. I want something better for
you.” You feel a sense of connection with that person, a deeper
rapport with that person.
And this is what you’re doing to a total stranger, through the internet,
just through the use of clever language.
Here’s an example. I say, “Listen,” anchor drop, “this is not what I
want for you. And I know it’s not what you desire for yourself.” Now,
I’m breaking one of the commandments here, I’m telling them what
they believe, because I am 100% positive that the believe this.
This is the only time you ever want to assume anything. When you are
100% positive that it’s the truth. No one on earth watching a video on
weight loss wants to have their body’s metabolism wrecked and their
entire life ruined. That’s kind of common sense, right?
So, it’s okay to say, “I know that’s not what you want for yourself.” I
changed the word want to desire. And why did it do that? And then I
put it in red, what leaps out at you when you see that? Desire. Desire
is one of those powerful words. I use it all the time. It’s in the power
words.
And I start by saying, “Listen.” Listen is a command word, it’s an NLP
drop word, an action drop. I am telling them what to do. Listen.
Whenever I say, “Listen,” you just kind of stop, don’t you?
It doesn’t matter what kind of conversation we’re having, if I say, “Hey,
listen.” There’s just a moment of silence there that’s just priceless, and
you’re going to do this with your VSL.
“Listen, this is not what I want for you. And I know it’s not what you
desire for yourself.” That’s a very powerful way to just simply say,
“That’s not where you’re at. This is completely wrong, and I know that
you don’t want that for yourself. So that is how you build the lie.
Let’s go to step 2, it’s not your fault. Let your viewer know that it’s
because of the lie that they’re not getting the results that they deserve.
It’s because of the lie, it’s not really their fault.
Now, later on, we’re going to come back and say, “It is now your
responsibility, because you know the truth.”
I’m going to use the weight loss as an example, but I could use any
industry as an example. “A lot of people believe that they just haven’t
starved themselves enough, or they haven’t been strong enough, or
haven’t had the will power enough. And all those things play a part,
sure. But the real culprit, the real reason for you not achieving your
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 8 of 9 --

9
desired result, is blank,” and that blank is the big lie. “It’s because you
believed this you’ve been led to believe it, like millions of other people
have, and it’s lead you down the wrong path.”
This is very important to say, and this is how I say it. “And this is the
reason that your struggle with weight loss is not your fault.” The words
“is not your fault,” which we’ve talked about before on a different video,
is so crucial. You let the reader temporarily off the guilt hook.
This builds rapport, this builds familiarity, this builds a lot of things that
are very, very psychologically powerful for you, when it comes to sales
and when it comes to rapport. “It’s not your fault.” Those are 4
magical words that you definitely want to use. In fact, they’re so
powerful, I’ve included it as a step.
So this is all there is to it, it’s just a variation on that phrase, “Look, this
isn’t your fault.”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
